elements_borrowed: Grid of small glass-door windows revealing individual food items, translated to cards displayed in a centered stack layout where each card is a 'window' into data
Coin-operated interaction model: the command palette is the 'coin slot' that opens windows to reveal content
Chrome and glass material language on card borders and hover states
Clean, efficient, minimal service counter aesthetic -- the top bar is the narrow service counter
elements_NOT_borrowed: Actual vending machine mechanics or coin-slot UI elements
Art Deco ornamental detailing from automat interiors

elements_borrowed: Ctrl+K activated modal with search input, grouped results, and keyboard navigation
Minimal shell approach where the command palette replaces traditional navigation entirely
Clean result items with icon + label + description pattern
elements_NOT_borrowed: Linear's dark theme or color scheme

elements_borrowed: Centered modal overlay with backdrop blur for command palette
Scale-in animation on open for the spotlight feel
elements_NOT_borrowed: Extension system or plugin architecture UI

elements_borrowed: Chrome gradient (light silver to darker silver) used on the top bar background
Subtle shine-sweep hover effect on buttons mimicking polished chrome reflection
3px chrome gradient strip at the top of cards (automat window frame trim)
elements_NOT_borrowed: Chrome stools, booths, or furniture shapes (explicitly in anti-repeat list)
Red/white color schemes typical of diners

elements_borrowed: Mint green (#e8f5e8) as the primary surface color, evoking 1950s kitchen and diner color trends
Cream (#f5f0e8) background evoking vintage ceramic tile
elements_NOT_borrowed: Avocado green or harvest gold specific to 1970s era

differences: Pass 1 uses diner booth / jukebox metaphor; Pass 8 uses automat vending wall
Pass 1 uses bottom jukebox navigation bar; Pass 8 uses command palette modal
Pass 1 uses pink/mint neon palette; Pass 8 uses mint green/cream/chrome
Pass 2 uses space-age / atomic swoops; Pass 8 uses clean, minimal automat lines
Pass 2 uses swooping top bar nav; Pass 8 uses flat minimal top bar + command palette
Pass 2 uses turquoise/coral palette; Pass 8 uses mint/cream/teal
Pass 3 uses cinema/film metaphor; Pass 8 uses automat restaurant metaphor
Pass 3 uses dark purple/yellow palette; Pass 8 uses light cream/mint
Pass 3 uses film-reel page transitions; Pass 8 uses scale-in modal transitions
Pass 4 uses soda fountain / floating dock; Pass 8 uses automat / command palette
Pass 4 uses cherry red/sky blue palette; Pass 8 uses teal/cream/chrome
Pass 4 uses receipt-roll scroll; Pass 8 uses standard centered scroll
Pass 5 also uses command palette navigation BUT with motel neon theme on dark background
Pass 5 uses dark asphalt (#1a1a1e) background; Pass 8 uses warm cream (#f5f0e8)
Pass 5 uses Permanent Marker + Cabin fonts; Pass 8 uses Righteous + Nunito
Pass 5 uses neon glow effects; Pass 8 uses chrome shine effects
Pass 5 road trip content persona; Pass 8 automat attendant content persona
Pass 6 uses kitchen appliance metaphor; Pass 8 uses automat restaurant metaphor
Pass 6 uses right-rail vertical dots timeline nav; Pass 8 uses command palette modal
Pass 6 uses avocado green/harvest gold; Pass 8 uses mint green/teal/chrome
Pass 6 uses Pacifico cursive heading; Pass 8 uses Righteous display heading
Pass 7 uses carnival carousel theme with floating pill nav; Pass 8 uses automat with command palette
Pass 7 uses Fredoka One + Quicksand fonts; Pass 8 uses Righteous + Nunito
Pass 7 uses confetti canvas ambient effect; Pass 8 uses chrome shimmer ambient effect
